Job Description
At Thermo Fisher Scientific, our mission is to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner, and safer. We are seeking a Senior Manager, Analytics & Insights to lead the strategy, development, and operation of our enterprise analytics capabilities.
This role will drive the analytics roadmap, deliver scalable data and reporting solutions, and advance a data-driven culture across the organization. The Senior Manager will partner closely with business and technology leaders while leading a global team of analytics professionals, data scientists, architects, and engineers to improve commercial performance, customer experience, supply chain operations, and strategic decision-making.
MAJOR JOB DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
Define and execute the enterprise analytics strategy, roadmap, and investment priorities.
Play a pivotal role in the modernization of Thermofisher Digital Analytics; roadmap migration to AI supported insights
Build scalable analytics and reporting capabilities that support customer, commercial, and operational objectives.
Establish a trusted, enterprise-wide data foundation that enables consistent reporting and decision-making.
Partner with business stakeholders to identify opportunities, solve complex problems, and deliver sustainable analytics solutions.
Generate actionable insights through analytics, experimentation, KPI reporting, and performance measurement.
Lead the design, implementation, and continuous improvement of cloud-based analytics platforms, data infrastructure, and data pipelines.
Establish standards for data architecture, governance, security, and analytics engineering.
Collaborate with product, architecture, and security teams to define requirements and prioritize initiatives.
Build, lead, and develop a high-performing global analytics organization of approximately 3 to 5 associates.
Foster a culture of innovation, continuous improvement, and data-driven decision-making.
Lead cross-functional analytics and data transformation programs while balancing competing business priorities.
Communicate analytics strategies, recommendations, and outcomes to executive leadership and key stakeholders.
Manage relationships with external vendors, consultants, and technology partners.
QUALIFICATIONS (Education/Training, Experience and Certifications)
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems, Business Analytics, or a related field; Master's degree preferred.
8+ years of experience in analytics, business intelligence, data engineering, data science, or related disciplines.
3+ years of leadership experience managing analytics, data engineering, or data science teams.
Proven success building and leading high-performing analytics organizations and enterprise-scale platforms.
Experience architecting, implementing, and supporting modern analytics infrastructure and cloud-based data solutions.
Expertise with analytics and visualization tools such as Tableau, Power BI, Google Analytics, or Adobe Analytics.
Experience with data warehouse technologies such as Snowflake, Teradata, Hadoop, or similar platforms.
Strong SQL skills and experience with Python, ETL/ELT development, and modern data integration practices.
Familiarity with AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud Platform; SAP and ERP integration experience preferred.
Experience developing AI-enabled analytics solutions, including agentic and conversational interfaces that improve access to data, insights, and decision-making across the enterprise preferred.
Knowledge of advanced analytics, machine learning, and predictive modeling preferred.
Strong communication, stakeholder management, problem-solving, and organizational leadership skills.
Ability to influence decision-making, manage competing priorities, and thrive in a fast-paced environment.
Up to 25% travel may be required.

Work Schedule
Standard (Mon-Fri)
Environmental Conditions
Office
Job Description
Senior Manager, Data Analytics and Pricing
Position Summary
The Senior Manager, Data Analytics and Pricing will support the Chemical Analysis Division (CAD) by leading data enablement, commercial analytics, pricing analytics, and related data-support. This role will partner closely with Finance, Commercial, IT, and Division leadership to translate business needs into scalable data solutions, actionable insights, and improved commercial execution.
This position will play a key role in strengthening CAD’s data-driven decision-making capabilities, building and improving data processes, analytics, and AI uses cases that support commercial performance, growth initiatives, pricing decisions, and operational effectiveness.
Key Responsibilities
Lead data analytics and pricing support for CAD, including data pipelines, analytical models, pricing analytics, and AI use cases.
Lead, coach, and develop a high-performing analytics team, including direct and indirect leadership of data and pricing analysts.
Partner with cross-functional stakeholders to translate business needs into actionable insights. Improve data models, reporting standards, automation, and adoption of analytics tools.
Build and improve data infrastructure, validation processes, and analytical workflows to strengthen data quality, accessibility, consistency, and speed of insight.
Develop and enhance tools and dashboards to measure key commercial and financial performance metrics, including revenue growth, margin performance, price impact, backlog management, customer/product trends, share gain, win/loss, and sales effectiveness.
Lead pricing analytics and pricing strategy execution, including price realization, annual price roll support, discount management, price setting, and performance tracking.
Provide data analytical support for monthly and quarterly business reviews, strategic planning, operating mechanisms, leadership reviews, and other division-level business priorities.
Drive stakeholder engagement, user training, tool adoption, documentation, and continuous improvement of analytics solutions.
Minimum Requirements / Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree in finance, business, mathematics, statistics, engineering, data analytics, or related field; advanced degree preferred.
8–10+ years of experience in finance, data analytics, strategy, pricing, business intelligence, data enablement, or related fields.
Prior experience leading finance or analytics teams, preferably in a matrixed and global organization.
Strong business acumen, analytical problem-solving, communication, and influencing skills, with ability to connect data and analytics to practical business outcomes.
Experience working with large, complex datasets and improving data quality, automation, reporting efficiency, and insight generation.
Ability to manage multiple priorities, lead through ambiguity, and operate effectively across functions and geographies.
Working knowledge of pricing, margin analysis, commercial performance management, promotions, discounting, or revenue growth management preferred.
Ability to travel as needed, approximately 10–15%.
Preferred Qualifications
Experience in life sciences, analytical instruments, diagnostics, industrial products, or other complex B2B environments.
Experience supporting commercial, finance, pricing, or product management organizations.
Familiarity with enterprise data platforms, CRM/ERP systems, sales reporting, pricing systems, and business intelligence environments.
This position is not eligible for employment visa sponsorship now or in the future. Applicants must be authorized to work in the United States without the need for sponsorship.

Work Schedule

Job Description
As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our global teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while helping to take science a step beyond by developing solutions for some of the world’s toughest challenges, like protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe or helping find cures for cancer.
How you will make an impact
The Senior Manager of R&D will lead a multidisciplinary engineering team responsible for the design, development, and commercialization of portable and stationary radiation detection, identification, and dosimetry products. This role will drive innovation from concept through product launch, while collaborating with other R&D teams to support the development and execution of key projects.
The ideal candidate combines strong technical depth with proven leadership capability, enabling effective cross-functional collaboration and delivery of complex, high-performance instrumentation in regulated environments.
---
What you will do
· Lead and develop a multidisciplinary R&D team (mechanical, electrical, firmware, systems) in the development of new products and maintenance of existing products.
· Drive end-to-end product development for handheld radiation detection and identification systems, from concept through commercialization.
· Define technical strategy, architecture, and development roadmaps aligned with business objectives.
· Partner cross-functionally with product management, manufacturing, quality, and regulatory teams to ensure successful product launches.
· Foster innovation in detection technologies, signal processing, and system performance.
· Ensure compliance with relevant regulatory standards and industry requirements (e.g., radiation safety, defense, homeland security).
· Establish and maintain engineering best practices, including design controls, documentation, and verification/validation processes.
---
How you will get here
Education & Experience
· MS or PhD in engineering, Physics, or related field
· 8+ years of R&D experience, including 3+ years leading technical teams.
· Proven track record delivering complex hardware/software products to market.
Desired Technical Skills
· Strong systems engineering mindset with experience integrating hardware, embedded firmware, and algorithms.
· Experience with ruggedized or field-deployed instrumentation.
· Background in radiation detection, nuclear instrumentation, or related physics-based systems.
· Knowledge of signal processing, data analysis, or isotope identification techniques.
· Experience with ruggedized or field-deployed systems.
---
Key Skills
· Leadership: Builds high-performing teams and drives accountability.
· Execution: Delivers complex programs on time in fast-paced environments.
· Customer Focus: Designs with mission success and end-user needs in mind.
· Collaboration: Influences across functions and communicates with impact.
· Innovation: Brings new ideas to advance performance and capability.

Work Schedule
Standard (Mon-Fri)
Environmental Conditions
Office
Job Description
As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our global teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while helping to take science a step beyond by developing solutions for some of the world’s toughest challenges, like protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe or helping find cures for cancer.
Location/Division Specific Information:
The Binding Site Group, part of Thermo Fisher Scientific, is a global leader in specialty diagnostics. We provide diagnostic assays and instruments to improve the diagnosis and management of blood cancers and immune system disorders. The Binding Site’s Freelite® offering is widely recommended for multiple myeloma diagnosis and monitoring across all stages of the disease. To learn more, visit  www.bindingsite.com .
Impactful Work:
The Quality and Regulatory Affairs team at Thermo Fisher Scientific is searching for a Senior Manager, Regulatory and Clinical Affairs to develop and execute regulatory strategy and clinical evidence generation plans, in partnership with Regulatory Affairs leadership, to support product claim expansions and regulatory submissions across the portfolio. This role will be responsible for recommending global regulatory pathways, identifying clinical evidence requirements, coordinating clinical study planning and execution, and ensuring that study outputs support high-quality submissions to regulatory authorities.
Key Responsibilities:
Regulatory Strategy and Submission Leadership
Develop and execute regulatory strategies for product claim expansions, new indications, expanded intended use, and new market access opportunities, in partnership with Regulatory Affairs leadership.
Recommend appropriate global regulatory pathways, submission requirements, evidence expectations, and agency interaction strategies for assigned programs.
Translate business priorities and product strategy into actionable regulatory plans, including timelines, risks, assumptions, and recommendations for decision points.
Lead and coordinate preparation of assigned regulatory submissions, responses to regulator questions, and supporting documentation packages, with oversight from Regulatory Affairs leadership as appropriate.
Assess product claims, intended use statements, labeling, promotional claims, and submission content to confirm they are supported by appropriate analytical, clinical, and scientific evidence.
Monitor evolving regulatory requirements and guidance relevant to clinical evidence, product claims, diagnostic performance, and market access, and communicate potential impact to project teams and leadership.
Clinical Affairs and Evidence Generation
Develop and execute clinical evidence strategies to support regulatory submissions for product claim expansions, New Product Development (NPD), and new market access, in partnership with Regulatory Affairs, Medical Affairs, and Biostatistics stakeholders.
Develop, coordinate, and oversee assigned clinical study plans, protocols, study designs, statistical analysis plans, clinical performance evaluation strategies, and clinical study reports.
Manage assigned clinical operations resources and external partners to ensure studies are delivered on time, within scope, and in compliance with applicable regulatory, quality, ethical, and data integrity requirements.
Coordinate clinical data review, monitoring, cleaning, analysis, and interpretation to ensure outputs are submission-ready.
Collaborate with clinical sites, investigators, CROs, laboratories, and internal stakeholders to support effective study startup, execution, closeout, and reporting.
Confirm clinical study documentation supports regulatory submissions, clinical performance claims, risk-benefit assessments, and post-market obligations.
Identify clinical evidence gaps and recommend practical strategies to close those gaps.
People and Program Leadership
Manage, coach, and develop assigned Regulatory Affairs and Clinical Affairs team members and/or project resources.
Lead cross-functional project teams responsible for claim expansion and clinical evidence programs.
Establish clear project plans, milestones, deliverables, owners, and escalation pathways.
Prepare and communicate program status, risks, decision recommendations, and trade-offs to leadership and governance forums.
Partner with Product Management and commercial stakeholders to evaluate and prioritize claim expansion opportunities based on customer need, regulatory feasibility, clinical evidence burden, cost, timeline, and business impact.
Partner with Quality and R&D to ensure regulatory and clinical requirements are integrated into design control, change control, risk management, and validation activities.
Influence stakeholders across functions and regions to support alignment, resolve issues, and maintain submission momentum.
Qualifications, Education and Experience:
Bachelor’s degree in life sciences, clinical research, regulatory affairs, biomedical science, medicine, or a related field; advanced degree preferred.
8+ years of experience with in vitro diagnostics, laboratory diagnostics, companion diagnostics, monitoring claims, or related diagnostic technologies; 10+ years preferred.
Experience with FDA submissions, including 510(k), De Novo, PMA supplements, Q-Submissions, or other regulatory interactions.
Experience with EU IVDR, clinical performance studies, performance evaluation reports, and/or global IVD regulatory requirements.
Experience managing or coordinating CROs, clinical sites, external laboratories, investigators, or clinical vendors.
Experience with biostatistics, clinical performance evaluation, analytical performance, risk management, and labeling/claims development.
Physical Requirements/Work Environment:
Office based role with one day hybrid working based on management discretion.
Ability to travel off-site for audits, training, workshops and team meetings
Our Mission:
Our mission is to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner, and safer. Join our team of 100,000+ colleagues, sharing our values of Integrity, Intensity, Innovation, and Involvement. Work with us to accelerate research, solve scientific challenges, drive innovation, and support patients in need. #StartYourStory at Thermo Fisher Scientific, where diverse experiences, backgrounds, and perspectives are valued.
Equal Opportunity Employer:
Thermo Fisher Scientific is an EEO/Affirmative Action Employer and does not discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status, disability, or any other legally protected status

Job Description
Senior Manager, Manufacturing Engineering
About Thermo Fisher Scientific
At Thermo Fisher Scientific, you'll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner, and safer. We empower our teams with the resources, development opportunities, and support needed to achieve individual career goals while advancing scientific innovation to solve some of the world's most complex challenges—from protecting the environment and ensuring food safety to accelerating the search for life-saving therapies.
Position Summary
The Senior Manager, Manufacturing Engineering will provide strategic and technical leadership to drive operational excellence, value engineering, and cost optimization across Thermo Fisher Scientific's external manufacturing network. This role is responsible for leading engineering-driven cost reduction initiatives, implementing a scalable Contract Manufacturer (CM) engagement framework, and developing a sustainable savings pipeline that supports business growth and profitability.
Working closely with Procurement, R&D, Operations, Finance, Supply Chain, and Contract Manufacturing partners, the successful candidate will identify and implement value creation opportunities, improve manufacturing efficiency, optimize product and process design, and ensure consistent execution of engineering best practices across the external manufacturing footprint.
Key Responsibilities
Contract Manufacturer Engagement & Continuous Improvement
Lead the deployment and execution of the Contract Manufacturer engagement framework, driving sustainable improvements in cost, quality, and operational performance.
Facilitate improvement workshops with Contract Manufacturers and cross-functional Thermo Fisher teams to identify and implement value engineering opportunities.
Evaluate cost-saving opportunities using established decision-making processes and contribute to performance tracking and reporting.
Monitor framework effectiveness, gather stakeholder feedback, and recommend enhancements to improve engagement outcomes.
Participate in and co-lead quarterly business reviews to assess performance, identify lessons learned, and drive continuous improvement.
Value Engineering & Cost Reduction
Lead and execute engineering-led cost reduction initiatives that contribute directly to company profitability and long-term competitiveness.
Manage and prioritize the value engineering opportunity pipeline, including: Design optimization and design control improvements
Make-versus-buy assessments
Manufacturing footprint optimization
Value chain and sourcing opportunities
Collaborate with internal and external partners to implement approved initiatives and ensure successful realization of savings.
Savings Pipeline Management
Develop, maintain, and manage a robust engineering savings pipeline aligned with business objectives.
Track project progress, quantify value creation, and communicate results to leadership and key stakeholders.
Ensure timely execution and realization of identified opportunities.
Cross-Functional Collaboration
Partner with Procurement, R&D, Product Management, Operations, and Supply Chain teams to implement strategic engineering and manufacturing improvements.
Collaborate with Finance to validate cost-saving opportunities, assess project feasibility, and ensure accurate savings reporting.
Support technology transfer, process optimization, and operational excellence initiatives across the external manufacturing network.
Performance Monitoring & Reporting
Establish, monitor, and report key performance indicators, including: Cost savings achieved
Project milestone completion
Return on investment (ROI)
Framework adoption and effectiveness
Present project outcomes, key achievements, and improvement opportunities to senior leadership and stakeholders.
Training & Capability Development
Develop and deliver training programs to ensure consistent application of the engagement framework and cost reduction methodologies.
Promote the adoption of Lean Manufacturing, Six Sigma, and Practical Process Improvement (PPI) principles across teams and external partners.
Share best practices and lessons learned to drive continuous capability development.
Minimum Qualifications
Advanced degree with 6+ years of experience, or Bachelor's degree with 8+ years of experience in regulated manufacturing environments such as medical device, pharmaceutical, diagnostics, or related industries.
Degree in Mechanical, Electrical, Manufacturing, Chemical Engineering, or a related technical discipline.
Minimum 5 years of leadership experience managing technical teams and leading organizational change initiatives.
Demonstrated success leading value engineering, cost reduction, continuous improvement, or operational excellence programs.
Strong understanding of cGMP, ISO standards, quality systems, and regulatory compliance requirements.
Experience with new product introductions, technology transfers, and process validation activities.
Expertise in Lean Manufacturing, Six Sigma methodologies, and Practical Process Improvement (PPI).
Strong project management capabilities with experience leading complex cross-functional initiatives.
Proven ability to develop and execute strategic plans aligned with business objectives.
Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and data-driven decision-making skills.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills with the ability to influence at all organizational levels.
Experience working with Contract Manufacturers and external suppliers is highly preferred.
Proficiency with manufacturing systems, quality tools, and engineering performance metrics.
Preferred Qualifications
Six Sigma Black Belt, PMP, or equivalent professional certification.
Experience in supplier development, contract manufacturing management, or external operations.
Knowledge of automation, digital manufacturing, and Industry 4.0 technologies.
Experience managing capital projects, budgets, and resource planning.
Travel
Travel required approximately 20–30%, including visits to Contract Manufacturing sites and key business partners.

Job Description
As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our global teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while helping to take science a step beyond by developing solutions for some of the world’s toughest challenges, like protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe or helping find cures for cancer.
DESCRIPTION:
Join our collaborative team as a Senior Manager of Strategy, where you'll contribute to shaping Thermo Fisher Scientific's future growth and success. You'll guide strategic initiatives that directly impact our mission of enabling customers to make the world healthier, cleaner, and safer. In this role, you'll lead the development and execution of comprehensive business strategies, working closely with executive leadership to identify and pursue growth opportunities across our global portfolio. You'll conduct in-depth market analysis, evaluate competitive landscapes, and translate insights into actionable recommendations that drive strategic decision-making. As a key member of our strategy team, you'll manage cross-functional projects, from initial concept through successful implementation, while building effective relationships across all organizational levels.
This role will lead the Innovation pillar of our Strategy team which is responsible for tracking the most important life science trends affecting our business. This team works with R&D leaders, subject matter experts, and business leaders across Thermo Fisher to identify opportunities to drive innovation. You'll conduct in-depth market analysis, evaluate competitive landscapes, and translate insights into actionable recommendations that drive strategic decision-making. As a key member of our strategy team, you'll manage cross-functional projects, from initial concept through successful implementation, while building effective relationships across all organizational levels.
KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:
Manage teams of subject matter experts tasked with identifying new scientific trends and technologies
Manage the Innovation Sensing team's resources and a team of up to 2 direct reports
Drive best practices for the Innovation Sensing team and cross-functional project teams
Communicate insights and emerging technology trends to key executive stakeholders
Partner, influence, and coordinate with senior R&D leaders to help shape LSG’s innovation agenda via Portfolio Reviews, Innovation Councils & R&D Forums
Partner and advise key business and strategy leaders to develop long-term innovation strategies to drive business growth
Define and drive market intelligence and customer research activities to help support strategic thinking and analysis
Establish working relationships with senior executives and R&D leaders across LSG and Thermo Fisher to understand their needs
Provide due diligence and analytical support on new technologies and M&A deals as needed
REQUIREMENTS:
Bachelor's degree in engineering, chemistry, biology, or related sciences
An MBA, advanced sciences degree, or 2+ years of experience in project engineering, project management, product management, or related experience in the Life Sciences industry
At least 2+ years experience leading teams preferred
Experience at a top-tier consulting, banking, or investing firm is a plus
Exceptional communication skills (esp. for executive-level audiences), and able to communicate clearly in group settings to influence actions and drive results within a matrixed environment
Strong understanding of life science research technologies, trends and competitive landscape
Strong organizational and program management skills
Excellence in data analysis, problem-solving, and strategic thinking
Strong understanding of basic finance and marketing terms and concepts
Able to identify and articulate the business case for emerging technologies
Able to manage concerns and conflicts productively and effectively within the core and extended teams
Demonstrated ability to lead teams and mentor colleagues in a high-paced, rapidly changing environment while maintaining order and a larger picture perspective.
Demonstrated ability to deliver results while working simultaneously on multiple projects, balancing resources, timing, and quality of outcomes
Experience with Lean / Process improvement
Compensation and Benefits
The salary range estimated for this position based in California is $143,000.00–$190,650.00.
This position may also be eligible to receive a variable annual bonus based on company, team, and/or individual performance results in accordance with company policy. We offer a comprehensive Total Rewards package that our U.S. colleagues and their families can count on, which includes:
A choice of national medical and dental plans, and a national vision plan, including health incentive programs
Employee assistance and family support programs, including commuter benefits and tuition reimbursement
At least 120 hours paid time off (PTO), 10 paid holidays annually, paid parental leave (3 weeks for bonding and 8 weeks for caregiver leave), accident and life insurance, and short- and long-term disability in accordance with company policy
Retirement and savings programs, such as our competitive 401(k) U.S. retirement savings plan
Employees’ Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP) offers eligible colleagues the opportunity to purchase company stock at a discount
For more information on our benefits, please visit: https://jobs.thermofisher.com/global/en/total-rewards
